comment: Brian (ElevenLabs) VO for the 300s Match 2 video — episode 2 of the series, same style as Ep1 (Mexico vs South Africa): real history and the story between the two nations. H2H facts: only friendlies ever played — 1989 Korea Cup 0-0 vs Czechoslovakia, 1998 Seoul 2-2, 2001 Prague 0-5 (Nedved; Korean press nicknamed new coach Hiddink 'Mr. Five-Zero'), 2002 Korea semi-final miracle, 2016 Prague 1-2 Korean revenge. First EVER World Cup meeting. `at` = start time in seconds; mux.mjs time-fits lines to their slots.

lines.text: Listen. Do you hear that? That is not just a crowd. That is thirty years of dreams... beating inside one man's chest.
Tonight, the greatest footballer Asia has ever produced begins his goodbye. And trust me... you are not ready for this story.
Welcome back to WorldCup twenty-six Legends — episode two. South Korea versus the Czech Republic. Group A. The Estadio Akron, Guadalajara.
Forty-eight thousand fans. Korean drums on one side, Czech songs on the other. And a secret hiding in plain sight...
In nearly a century of football, these two nations have NEVER met at a World Cup. Not once. Four meetings, all friendlies... and every one of them left a mark.
Seoul, nineteen ninety-eight: a wild two-all draw that settled nothing. Then Prague, two thousand and one... Pavel Nedved and the Czechs crushed Korea five-nil.
The humiliation cut so deep that Korean newspapers gave their brand-new coach a cruel nickname: Mister Five-Zero. That coach... was Guus Hiddink.
One year later, those same wounded warriors stunned Italy, shocked Spain, and danced into the semi-finals of their own World Cup. The five-nil didn't break Korea. It built the miracle.
And in twenty-sixteen, in Prague, Korea finally took revenge — two-one. The scar was settled. But the World Cup chapter was never written... until tonight.
So meet tonight's South Korea. Nine straight World Cups. Five warriors carry the flame.
Son Heung-min. Thirty-three years old. His fourth World Cup — and his last. Every touch, every sprint, every shot is now a goodbye written in lightning.
Behind him stands The Monster. Kim Min-jae — the wall that conquered Italy with Napoli. Strikers see him in their nightmares.
And pulling the strings, Lee Kang-in — the magician from Paris who turns defenders into spectators.
But the Czechs are heirs to a dynasty. Two World Cup finals. A European title. And the Panenka — the most audacious penalty football has ever seen. Audacity runs in this bloodline.
Patrik Schick — the marksman who scored from the halfway line at a European Championship. He lives in the margins, and he has already calculated every possible outcome.
Tomas Soucek, the tower of Prague — two metres of midfield iron. And Adam Hlozek, the young wolf, hungry to drag Czech football back into the light.
Every great match hides a secret duel inside it. The Monster... against the Marksman. Kim Min-jae against Patrik Schick. Ninety minutes. One cage. One escape artist.
One of them will own this night. The other will dream about it for the rest of his life.
Our storytellers have looked into tonight... and this is what they saw.
Forty-first minute. Son receives on the left. He cuts inside — the move that has beaten a thousand defenders — and he lets it fly.
GOAL! Top corner! Sonaldo! In the first dance of his farewell, the old magic makes time itself stand still!
Look at these faces. Tears of pure joy. This is what football does — it turns grown men back into children.
But the Czechs rise. Schick drifts wide, escapes the Monster for the very first time all night... and the whole stadium forgets how to breathe.
And out of nowhere — Hwang Hee-chan! The tackle of a lifetime! The ball spins away... and with it, the Czech dream.
Full time. One, nil. Twenty-five years after Mister Five-Zero... the rematch that mattered belongs to Korea. A warrior's farewell begins with victory.
Three points for South Korea. A goal for the captain. And for the Czechs — two more chances to write a different ending.
That is the beauty of Group A. Nothing is decided... and everything is still possible.
Now here is where YOU come in. Did you see this coming? At WorldCup twenty-six dot world, you pick three of the forty-eight nations — and every single goal they score... scores for you.
Underdogs multiply your points. The leaderboard moves live with every goal. And it is completely free to play. Your three picks could change everything.

If this story gave you goosebumps — smash that like, share it with a football romantic, and subscribe. Because next, Canada opens its World Cup... and you do NOT want to miss what our storytellers have seen.
